title: Use Place Value to Add
essential_question: How can you use place value to add 3-digit numbers?

pretest.question: Add. 423 + 165 = ?

expected_errors.note: A: 3+5=8, 2+6=8, 4+1=5 → 588 not 578 (off by 10 in ones/tens confusion)

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11 — 'Even students who use correct decomposition strategies may revert to a digit-by-digit view when combining partial sums, omitting regrouping across the boundary.'
expected_errors.note: D: 4+0+88=488 — dropped hundreds of second addend (regroup omission in hundreds)

pretest.hint: Add ones, tens, hundreds from right to left.

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

expected_errors.note: A: 6+8=14, carry 1, 5+1+1=7, 3+2=5 → 574 not 564 (forgot to apply carry to tens)

expected_errors.citation: NCTM NBT Progressions p.11 — 'Even students who use correct decomposition strategies may revert to a digit-by-digit view when combining partial sums, omitting regrouping across the boundary.'
expected_errors.note: D: forgot to carry ones to tens → 474 (hundreds and ones correct, carry omitted)

expected_errors.note: A: 2+1=3, 7+6=13 carry 1, 4+3+1=8 → 833 not 733 (hundreds digit off by 1)
C: 833 not 843 — student added extra ten in tens column
D: incorrect regrouping — swapped carry direction

expected_errors.note: C: forgot to carry from tens → 743 (hundreds digit one less than correct)
D: swapped ones digit in answer

learn.title: The Place Value Algorithm
learn.content: The place value method for addition means lining up numbers by their place values (ones, tens, hundreds) and adding each column from right to left. When a column adds to 10 or more, you regroup.

visual_data.description: Base-ten blocks being combined, with regrouping shown as 10 ones being traded for 1 ten rod

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_note: Place value algorithm: align ones/tens/hundreds, add right to left

learn.title: Step 1: Add the Ones
learn.content: Line up the numbers vertically. Start with the ones column. Example: 356 + 218. Ones: 6+8=14. Since 14≥10, write 4 in the ones place and regroup 1 ten to the tens column.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_note: Step 1 — ones: 6+8=14, write 4 in ones place, carry 1 to tens

learn.title: Step 2: Add the Tens
learn.content: Now add the tens column, including any regrouped ten. Example: 356 + 218. Tens: 5+1+1(carried)=7. Write 7 in the tens place. No regrouping needed here.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_note: Step 2 — tens: 5+1+1(carry)=7, write 7 in tens place

learn.title: Step 3: Add the Hundreds
learn.content: Finally add the hundreds column. Example: 356 + 218. Hundreds: 3+2=5. Write 5. The answer is 574. Check with estimate: 400+200=600. Is 574 close? Yes!

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_note: Step 3 — hundreds: 3+2=5, write 5 in hundreds place → 574

learn.title: Regrouping Tens to Hundreds
learn.content: Sometimes the tens add to 10 or more. Example: 472 + 361. Ones: 2+1=3. Tens: 7+6=13. Write 3 tens, regroup 1 hundred. Hundreds: 4+3+1=8. Answer: 833.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_note: Regrouping tens→hundreds: e.g. 472+361, 7+6=13 carry 1 to hundreds

learn.title: Double Regrouping
learn.content: Some problems need regrouping in both ones and tens! Example: 487 + 356. Ones: 7+6=13 → write 3, carry 1. Tens: 8+5+1=14 → write 4, carry 1. Hundreds: 4+3+1=8. Answer: 843.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_note: Double regroup: 487+356, carry from ones AND from tens — track both carries

learn.title: Adding Three Numbers
learn.content: You can use the same steps with three addends. Example: 140 + 457 + 301. Ones: 0+7+1=8. Tens: 4+5+0=9. Hundreds: 1+4+3=8. Answer: 898. Estimate: 150+450+300=900. Close!

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_talk_prompt: When adding three numbers (215+348+126), you got carries from the ones AND the tens column. How did you keep track of both carries at the same time? What would happen if you lost track of one carry? Explain a strategy for making sure you never miss a carry.

learn.title: Key Idea Summary
learn.content: Use place value to add: 1) Estimate first. 2) Line up by place value. 3) Add ones — regroup if 10 or more. 4) Add tens — regroup if 10 or more. 5) Add hundreds. 6) Check: Is your answer close to the estimate?

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.29-34

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

learn.math_note: Summary: align → ones → tens → hundreds → regroup each place as needed

practice_r3.question: Jake added 478 + 356: 'Ones: 8+6=14, write 4 carry 1. Tens: 7+5=12, write 2 carry 1. Hundreds: 4+3+1=8. Answer: 824.' What mistake did Jake make?

choices.A: He forgot to add the carried 1 in the tens column
choices.D: He made no mistake — 824 is correct

expected_errors.note: B: 8+6=14 is correct (Jake's ones column is right)
C: hundreds (4+3+1=8) depends on carry from tens; error is in tens, not hundreds
D: correct answer is 834, not 824 — Jake's work has an error

solution_steps: Jake forgot to add the carried 1
practice_r3.hint: Check whether Jake added the carried 1 in the tens.
practice_r3.math_note: 478+356=834; Jake's error: 7+5=12 carry 1 (correct), forgot to add carry in tens column → wrote 824

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

practice_r3.question: Without calculating, which sum is greater: 456 + 378 or 463 + 375?

expected_errors.note: A: 456+378=834 < 463+375=838 — A is less, not greater (answer is B)
D: can reason by inspection — same total digit sum, compare last two digits

solution_steps: Quick check: both have same hundreds total (4+3=7 plus carries)
But 463+375 has a cleaner ones (8 vs 14), meaning less gets 'lost' to carrying
practice_r3.hint: Try estimating or think about how the digits change.

practice_r3.question: A school has $575 in its fund. They earn $368 from a bake sale and $279 from a car wash. Do they have enough for a $1,200 field trip?

expected_errors.note: B: total is $1,222, not $1,122 (carry error in tens column)
C: total is $1,222, not $1,322 (added extra hundred)
D: total is $1,222, not $1,022 (forgot carry from tens to hundreds)

practice_r3.hint: Add all three amounts, then compare to $1,200.

practice_r3.question: Which is TRUE about the place value method and the break apart method?

choices.B: Place value works only without regrouping
choices.C: They both use place value and always give the same answer
choices.D: Break apart is faster for all problems

expected_errors.citation: Smarter Balanced G3 Item Specs 2015 — 'Strategy selection is explicitly tested at Grade 3; students must identify the most efficient strategy for a given number pair, not just apply any valid strategy.'
expected_errors.note: A: both strategies always produce the same (correct) answer — they never differ

expected_errors.citation: Smarter Balanced G3 Item Specs 2015 — 'Strategy selection is explicitly tested at Grade 3; students must identify the most efficient strategy for a given number pair, not just apply any valid strategy.'
expected_errors.note: B: place value (column addition) works WITH regrouping — that is its core feature
D: break apart is not universally faster; speed depends on number structure and student

solution_steps: The place value method (column addition) adds ones, tens, hundreds vertically
The break apart method separates into partial sums, then combines
Both always give the same correct answer
The methods are different approaches to the same concept
practice_r3.hint: Try both methods on the same problem. What do you notice?
practice_r3.math_note: Both place-value and break-apart strategies apply place-value understanding and produce identical correct answers

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 7 p.34 SMARTER / Enrichment

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 5-6 — standard algorithm addition with regrouping

assessment_source.name: NCTM Progressions NBT K-5 p.11-12 — column addition and regrouping

unit_intro_message: 이 단원에서는 세 자리 수 덧셈과 뺄셈을 1,000까지 확장합니다. 자리값 알고리즘(세로셈)으로 자리올림을 단계별로 처리하는 방법을 익히세요.
unit_close_message: 단원을 마쳤습니다! 자리값 알고리즘의 핵심은 각 자리를 오른쪽부터 더하고, 합이 9를 초과하면 다음 자리로 올려주는 것입니다.
